hook.text: Eighty percent of the world's population relies on traditional medicine. It's not alternative — it's the mainstream.

buildup.text: Traditional medicine includes herbal remedies, spiritual healing, and bodywork passed down through generations.

discovery.text: These systems often treat the whole person — body, mind, spirit, and social relations — not just isolated symptoms.

twist.text: Many modern drugs owe debts to traditional knowledge. Aspirin from willow bark, quinine from cinchona bark.

climax.text: Traditional medicine isn't outdated. It's a living system of knowledge that biomedicine is only beginning to respect.

punchline.text: The oldest medicine is still the most widely used.

quiz.question: How much of the world uses traditional medicine?
